summary: The Optimism RPC block-loading divergence is semicolon-only rustfmt noise at the end of an explicit return chain; no block/receipt loading behavior changed.
notes: Queue entry verified as line 224: M	crates/optimism/rpc/src/eth/block.rs.
Context from the source tree confirms the `.collect::<Result<Vec<_>, Self::Error>>().map(Some);` expression is preceded by `return block...`, so adding the semicolon does not alter return behavior.
No transaction/receipt zipping, L1 cost cache clearing, receipt building, or empty result behavior changed.
